qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code injection via unsanitized OpenAPI pattern field in mock generation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets packages/mock/src/faker/getters/scalar.ts pattern interpolation into a single-quoted faker.fromRegExp string, not x-enumDescriptions emitted into JavaScript comments through jsStringEscape.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via disabled safeUrlResolver in OpenAPI spec $ref resolution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets HTTP $ref resolution with safeUrlResolver disabled, not generated-code comment escaping for x-enumDescriptions.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns unsanitized x-enumDescriptions inserted into JavaScript comment blocks because jsStringEscape fails to escape '*' and '/', allowing a '*/' comment breakout and code injection. The session's qualifying findings cover a separate pattern-field string interpolation bug and a separate SSRF resolver configuration bug.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gch2-phqh-fg9q describes @orval/core code injection through x-enumDescriptions, JavaScript comments, and jsStringEscape not sanitizing '*' or '/'.
ssrf_safe_url_resolver/vulnerability_report.json describes packages/orval/src/utils/http-resolver.ts setting safeUrlResolver: false for $ref HTTP resolution.
Session discovery logs list only code_injection_pattern_field and ssrf_safe_url_resolver as the two qualifying findings.
